subject: Project Management
title: Waterfall's Iron Grip
description: A short lesson to help you apply Waterfall's Iron Grip.

hook.text: For 30 years, nearly every software project followed the same rigid sequence: plan, build, test.

buildup.text: Waterfall felt safe. You planned everything upfront, then executed. Simple in theory.

discovery.text: In practice, requirements changed constantly. By delivery, the software was already obsolete.

twist.text: The U.S. Department of Defense made waterfall mandatory — locking the industry into rigidity.

climax.text: An entire generation of developers knew only one way to build software — and it was broken.

punchline.text: The standard was standard because nobody questioned it.

quiz.question: Why did waterfall persist for so long despite its flaws?
options: It was mandated by the U.S. Department of Defense
It actually worked well for software
